question: When conducting a public procurement procedure, can a contracting authority (e.g. a Ministry) be seen as a 'national authority' that, in line with the Kolin judgment, is not empowered to take decisions concerning access by economic operators from non-covered countries?
topic: Public procurement — exclusive EU competence over access of third-country operators (Kolin)
gold_answer: Only the EU has competence to adopt an act of GENERAL APPLICATION concerning access of third-country economic operators to EU public procurement (Kolin, C-652/22, paras 60-61). A Ministry conducting a single procurement procedure acts as a contracting authority in that individual procedure, which is distinct from adopting acts of general application; the Kolin limit targets the latter.
source: Amicus_2.docx — expert legal memo (section II), provided 2026-06-02
notes: corpus_gap: Kolin (C-652/22, Oct 2024) is NOT in the corpus, though it is cited by cases that are. This entry measures behaviour when the controlling authority is absent; excluded from the main aggregate.
